user_query:
I’m an insurance agent batching primary care reimbursement work. Using the provided inputs, please (1) identify which claims need provider action, (2) generate a structured CSV for our internal queue, (3) rewrite our rough notification template for tone/clarity/brevity while preserving placeholders, and (4) produce per‑provider notice drafts from the polished template.

Inputs (all located under input/):
- input/claims.csv
- input/providers.csv
- input/notification_template.txt

Actionability rules (apply to each row in claims.csv):
A claim requires provider action if any of the following are true: missing_docs = "yes" OR status is "Denied" OR status is "Pending" OR paid_amount < allowed_amount.

Derived fields:
- issues: include any of the following labels that apply, joined by "; ": "Missing documentation" (missing_docs = yes), "Denied" (status = Denied), "Pending" (status = Pending), "Underpayment" (paid_amount < allowed_amount).
- reimbursement_due: max(allowed_amount − paid_amount, 0), rounded to two decimals.

Required outputs:
1) output/claim_actions.csv
   - Columns (in this exact order): provider_id, provider_name, claim_id, patient_id, service_date, issues, reimbursement_due
   - Include only claims requiring provider action.
   - Sort by provider_id ascending, then claim_id ascending.
   - reimbursement_due should be numeric with two decimals (no currency symbol).

2) output/notification_template_polished.txt
   - Rewrite input/notification_template.txt for a professional, courteous, neutral tone; remove harsh language and redundancies; keep it concise.
   - Keep the following placeholders unchanged and present: {PROVIDER_NAME}, {TOTAL_DUE}, {CLAIM_TABLE}, {RESPONSE_DEADLINE}.
   - Include a Subject line that begins with "Subject:" and a brief closing/signature.
   - The body (excluding placeholders) should be at most 120 words.

3) output/messages/{provider_id}_notice.txt
   - Create one message per provider that has at least one claim requiring action; do not create a file for providers with no such claims.
   - Use output/notification_template_polished.txt as the base. Replace placeholders as follows:
     • {PROVIDER_NAME} → provider_name from providers.csv
     • {TOTAL_DUE} → sum of reimbursement_due for that provider, formatted as "$" + two decimals (e.g., $125.00)
     • {RESPONSE_DEADLINE} → the literal text "10 business days from notice date"
     • {CLAIM_TABLE} → one line per applicable claim in the format: "claim_id | patient_id | service_date | issues | $reimbursement_due". Sort lines by claim_id ascending. Use two decimals and a leading "$" for reimbursement_due.
   - Cross‑check that the sum of the listed reimbursement_due values exactly matches {TOTAL_DUE} in the message.

General constraints:
- Do not include claims that fail the actionability rules.
- Use only the provided inputs; do not access external systems.
- Ensure the outputs are reproducible and self‑contained under the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
